tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'optical shop Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto's optical shops might have better selection than Tokyo — and you'll hit them first while fresh.
tasks.reward: Removes the 'where do I even buy glasses' anxiety before landing in Japan.
nuggets: Japan glasses ~¥15,000-25,000 per pair
Same-day service common in major cities
Bring prescription or get eye test there
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm arriving March 25 and want to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses early in the trip. I need stores that: 1) serve English speakers or have easy non-verbal service, 2) offer same-day or next-day service, 3) have good selection in the ¥15,000-25,000 range. Provide a list with Google Maps links, neighborhood locations, and any notes about their specialties or tourist-friendliness. Prioritize stores near central Kyoto hotels.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance: 'Do you cover glasses purchased in Japan?'
tasks.hook_line: 4 pairs in Japan could save $400+ vs US prices — if insurance plays along.
tasks.reward: Know whether you're paying out-of-pocket or getting reimbursed before you buy.
nuggets: US insurance often covers foreign glasses
Need itemized receipt in English
Prescription valid 1-2 years in most states
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out insurance coverage for buying prescription eyeglasses in Japan. I'm on a US vision insurance plan and want to buy 4 pairs during my March trip. What questions should I ask my insurance provider? What documentation will I need to get reimbursed (receipts, prescription format, etc.)? Also clarify: can I use my US prescription in Japan, or do I need a new eye exam there?

tasks.first_domino: Search 'best temples Kyoto not crowded' and open tabs
tasks.hook_line: 8 days out and Kyoto is still a blank page — it's your longest stop.
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left.
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, best at sunrise
Arashiyama bamboo grove crowds thin before 8am
Philosopher's Path ~2km walk, cherry blossoms late March
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, quiet neighborhoods, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with: opening hours, Google Maps links, estimated walking times, and a mix of major landmarks and less touristy spots. Prioritize early mornings to avoid crowds. Include restaurant recommendations for lunch and dinner.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama day trips hiking' and read top 3 results
tasks.hook_line: Takayama's the mountain town wildcard — less famous than Kyoto, might be the highlight.
tasks.reward: Locks in the Alps portion of the trip — nature break between temple cities.
nuggets: Shirakawa-go village 1hr bus, UNESCO site
Takayama morning market 7am-noon, local crafts
Old Town preserved Edo-period streets
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2 days in Takayama, Japan (late March 2026). Traveling with Dana. We want a mix of hiking/nature and exploring the old town. Looking for: 1-2 day trips from Takayama (like Shirakawa-go or nearby trails), best times to visit the morning market, good local restaurants, and any hidden gems locals recommend. Provide Google Maps links and realistic time estimates for getting between places.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Tokyo neighborhoods guide' and bookmark top article
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo has 14 million people and infinite options — which version of Tokyo do you actually want?
tasks.reward: Completes the Japan trip planning trifecta — flights, hotels, activities all done.
nuggets: Tsukiji Outer Market sushi breakfast ~¥2000
TeamLab Borderless tickets sell out weeks ahead
Shimokitazawa vintage shopping, indie cafes
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 4 days in Tokyo (early April 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like: good food (sushi, ramen, izakayas), walking interesting neighborhoods, outdoor spaces, and a little nightlife. Already have central Tokyo hotel. I want a day-by-day breakdown with: neighborhood focus, key activities, restaurant recs, and realistic travel times between spots. Include a mix of famous sites and local areas. Also flag anything that needs advance tickets.

tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Google 'warm backpacking March California desert' and scan options
tasks.hook_line: First time planning a backpacking trip — deserts, coast, or mountains?
tasks.reward: Turns a birthday weekend into an adventure you'll actually remember.
nuggets: Joshua Tree ~70°F mid-March, permits easy
Big Sur coast trails mild, book campsites ahead
Anza-Borrego desert wildflowers peak March
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm 1-2 night backpacking destination for mid-March (around March 14-15). I'm based in the US and willing to fly somewhere warm. Looking for: temps above 60°F, beginner-friendly trails (I'm renting gear from REI for the first time), scenic camping, and easy permits. Prioritize California deserts or Southwest options. Give me 3-4 options with trail names, distance, elevation gain, permit requirements, and nearest airports.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and plug in the destination from task 1
tasks.hook_line: Once you pick the spot, booking is the easy dopamine hit.
tasks.reward: Makes the trip real — no more 'maybe we'll do this' energy.
nuggets: Southwest flies to Palm Springs, bags free
Book Thursday night to maximize weekend
REI gear pickup same-day with reservation
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book flights for a backpacking trip March 14-15 (or 13-15 if overnight Thursday makes sense). Once I've picked a destination, help me find the best flight options: cheap but not painful, ideally direct, and timed so I can pick up REI rental gear before heading to the trailhead. I'll need to get back to an airport by Sunday afternoon to catch a flight to Chicago.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Search flights from [destination airport] to Chicago on March 15-16
tasks.hook_line: Bridge flight from wilderness to birthday party — logistics puzzle unlocked.
tasks.reward: Completes the travel Tetris — all movement booked.
nuggets: Chicago ORD has more options than Midway
Sunday afternoon arrival gives party prep time
One-way flights often same price as roundtrip
tasks.system_prompt: Help me book a flight from [backpacking destination airport] to Chicago for Mariano's 30th birthday (March 19). I need to arrive in Chicago by Sunday evening (March 15 or 16) to have time before the party. Find the best options that connect with my backpacking trip logistics. I already have a return flight from Chicago, just need this one-way.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Go to REI.com and click 'Adventures' then 'Rental Gear'
tasks.hook_line: REI rents full backpacking kits — can you actually pull off a trip without buying $800 of gear?
tasks.reward: Gets the gear question answered so you can stop worrying about what you need.
nuggets: REI backpacking package ~$50-80 for weekend
Includes pack, tent, sleeping bag, pad
Reserve online, pickup at local store day-of
tasks.system_prompt: Help me understand REI's gear rental program for a 1-2 night backpacking trip in mid-March. I need: backpack, tent (2-person), sleeping bag (warm enough for 40-50°F nights), and sleeping pad. What's available, how much does it cost, how far in advance do I need to reserve, and can I pick it up the day of my trip? Also tell me what I still need to bring myself (clothes, food, water, etc.).

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano: 'What do you actually want for your 30th?'
tasks.hook_line: What do you get someone turning 30 when you're already flying to their party?
tasks.reward: Removes the 'should I bring something' mental loop before the trip.
nuggets: Experience gifts > stuff for milestone birthdays
Chicago food scene: gift card to Alinea ~$200
Whiskey flights at Longman & Eagle ~$30
tasks.system_prompt: Help me brainstorm a 30th birthday gift for my friend Mariano. I'm already flying to Chicago for his party, so presence is covered. He's into good food, nightlife, and trying new things. Budget ~$50-150. Should I go with an experience (restaurant gift card, activity), something personal, or just skip it since I'm traveling for the party? Give me 3-4 solid options with reasoning.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the repo and run 'npm install' to see what breaks
tasks.hook_line: Developer portal for developers that doesn't run on your dev machine — irony you can fix.
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work and gets you back in the codebase after months parked.
nuggets: Last touched Feb, likely dependency drift
Internal portal = auth setup probably needed
Check Slack for 'running Cerebro locally' threads
tasks.system_prompt: I'm trying to get Cerebro (internal developer portal) running locally after being away from it for months. It's a Node.js project, likely React-based. Walk me through: 1) common setup issues for internal tools (auth, env vars, database connections), 2) debugging steps when 'npm install' or 'npm start' fails, 3) where to look for internal documentation (README, Confluence, Slack channels), and 4) how to ask for help without looking clueless. Assume I have standard dev tools installed but haven't touched this codebase since February.

recommended_slots.reason: Weekend morning ramp-up energy, perfect for research with novelty hook — backpacking trip is TODAY's deadline
Low-effort research batch pairs well with task 1, both travel planning, curiosity-driven, 8 days out
Quick win errand, low context cost, unblocks backpacking logistics — palette cleanser before deeper Japan planning
